players.name: DJX95

notes: Their main damage was Murdock (49k physical); 2/5 of you itemized armor.
Enemy ran 2/5 on or near their meta core.

draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (support→offlane).

coaching.headline: A 39-minute grind you nearly threw, saved by finally winning the Orb Prime fight at 35.5.
coaching.team: Both mid and carry were read as theirs all game, and Murdock (49k physical) was a monster — they out-killed you on objectives 27 to 22. You lost a long string of fights to him: the 12.2 Fangtooth (1-4), the 20.3 Fangtooth (0-2), a 23.7 open-map bad trade (0-3), and the 27.4 Orb Prime fight (1-4). What saved it was refusing to keep flipping the same fight and instead grouping late — you out-damaged them on objectives overall (92k to 42k) and won the 35.5 Orb Prime fight (3-2) and the 38.1 close (3-0). The lesson is the 23.7 bad trade: a 3-man into Murdock and Crunch in the open with nothing up was a free loss. Two of you built armor against 49k physical — that should have been three.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Surviving a long run of losses to a 49k-damage Murdock and finally winning the 35.5 Orb Prime fight (3-2) plus the 38.1 close — after the 27.4 Orb Prime loss (1-4) nearly ended it.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Phase died 6 times, three times your norm, mostly into Murdock in the lost fights (12.2, 27.4). As the frontline support facing a 49k carry, your peel had to be on your own carries, not on the front line of dives. The win came when the team stopped forcing those fights — your job is to call that earlier.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Best US game — Greystone 5/3/11, 40k hero damage, 46k mitigated, and you won the 13.6 Fangtooth pick (2-0). You were the steady frontline that let the late grouping work. The 35.5 Orb Prime win that saved it ran through you. Keep being the anchor that doesn't over-commit.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Sparrow was the unfavored carry into Murdock (theirs all game) and you still hit 11.2 CS/min, above your norm, and 21k into objectives. 4 deaths is clean for an unfavored lane. You were in the 23.7 bad trade though — with the lane behind, sit back and let the frontline absorb Murdock before you commit.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Lt. Belica top-damaged the team (45k) but died 8 times, well over your norm. You were in the 12.2 and 27.4 losses to Murdock. As a back-line setup mid, 8 deaths means you were positioning too aggressively into a fed physical carry. Land your stun from range and stay out of his reach.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Khaimera was the objective engine — 49k damage to objectives is enormous and it's why you out-damaged them on majors despite the kill deficit. 7 deaths and low hero damage (348/min) say you were diving for camps more than fighting; in a game decided by team fights vs Murdock, balance the objective focus with being present for the 35.5 win.

threats: Chain-CC risk (3 hard-CC abilities, ~2.8s of lockdown): Crunch Right Crunch (knockup 1.25s), Iggy & Scorch Inferno (knockback), Akeron Web Lash (root 1s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Muriel. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — you brought none.
Dive threat on your backline: Crunch. Assign peel for your carry and ward your flanks.
synergy: Your engage/CC: Lt. Belica Neural Disruptor (silence 0.5s). Your kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
You have sustain (Phase) — favor the longer fight where your heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Telekinetic Link an ally → Psychic Flare blind → Energy Lance → Hyperflux buff ult
ourKits.winCondition: buff a hyper-carry through the Link and Hyperflux for a teamfight
ourKits.passive: abilities heal her and her linked ally
ourKits.playWith: the strongest carry to Link and supercharge
ourKits.playAgainst: kill the linked carry, not Phase; break the link timing

ourKits.combo: Assault The Gates leap → Make Way armor shred → Sacred Oath empowered basic → Stone Forged Soul stasis ult
ourKits.winCondition: front-line aggressively and reset the fight with the stasis heal
ourKits.playWith: a team that fights while he soaks and stasis-resets
ourKits.playAgainst: don't waste burst into his stasis — wait it out; anti-heal cuts the ult heal

ourKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
ourKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
ourKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
ourKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

ourKits.combo: Seismic Assault knockup → Void Bomb → Neural Disruptor silence ult, Void Drone mana drain
ourKits.winCondition: knock up + silence a target and burst with missing-HP scaling
ourKits.playAgainst: dodge Seismic Assault; she's immobile — dive when ult is down

ourKits.combo: Ambush leap → Unleash AS → Cull stun, Invigorate to cleanse+heal
ourKits.winCondition: stick on one target, stun with Cull and out-sustain via Spirit Regen
ourKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his Cull stun
ourKits.playAgainst: ANTI-HEAL is mandatory — it guts his whole kit; kite the leap and CC through Invigorate

enemyKits.combo: Static Trap root → Buckshot → Hot Pursuit AS → Long Arm of the Law global snipe
enemyKits.winCondition: trap for the root, then out-range with Shots Fired and finish with the global ult
enemyKits.passive: every 5th basic is a long-range bonus shot
enemyKits.playWith: frontline/peel to protect his low mobility
enemyKits.playAgainst: almost no escape — engage past the trap and he dies

enemyKits.combo: Alacrity shield+AS → Consecrated Ground bubble → Reversal global save
enemyKits.winCondition: keep the carry alive with global shields and enable their damage
enemyKits.passive: abilities grant Sentinel to allies; on-hit bonus
enemyKits.playAgainst: she has no damage threat — dive her carry through the shields or pick her first

enemyKits.combo: Forward Crunch gap-close stun → Right Crunch knockup → empowered Left Crunch, sustain via Re-Crunch
enemyKits.winCondition: all-in a squishy with the empowered combo and heal back through Re-Crunch
enemyKits.passive: post-ability empowered basic that refunds cooldowns
enemyKits.playWith: peel-stripping engage so he reaches the backline
enemyKits.playAgainst: peel/CC him off — without resets he's stranded; anti-heal blunts Re-Crunch

enemyKits.combo: Oil Spill → Molotov ignite → Flame Turret → Inferno knockback ult
enemyKits.winCondition: layer fire zones on a choke/objective and burn the team
enemyKits.playWith: CC that holds enemies in his fire
enemyKits.playAgainst: don't fight in his zones — flank; kill the turret, dive him

enemyKits.combo: Web Lash root → Crushing Maw armor shred → Hellfire eye → Consume pull-channel
enemyKits.winCondition: root with Web Lash and grind with Hunger-stacked haste
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up burst on his rooted target
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge Web Lash — without the root he can't lock you; interrupt Consume

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
